children.title:
Overview / Goal

Build a web app that has intelligent, flexible scheduling for the user. This means allowing them to set recurring tasks, push tasks off if another task goes long, etc...

The goal is to provide a framework for the user that keeps them on track with applying spaced repetition to their learning objectives, affords them enough time and space to push through and finish tasks that require a relatively indeterminate time to complete, and tracks / reminds them of long running repeated tasks that they likely need to do. The long running / repeated tasks should enter time range phases. Something like "upcoming", "needs doing", "time to do", "overdue". Should have appropriate coloring (ie overdue is red).

It should allow setting the range of work times the user wants to work during the week and then appropriately fill in tasks that can / should be accomplished in order to not lose momentum in that area of expertise. 

Tasks should be intelligently classified as those "needing appropriate build up time to become productive / efficient" and those one can jump in and out of with relative ease. The ones that could be jumped in and out of can act as filler time blocks in the week that is otherwise filled with long time blocks of the former tasks.

Extremely urgent tasks should be able to be added at any time with some sort of "overtime" option that expands that specific task into non-working hours of the day. If a task like this is created, it should automatically recompute what the future schedule would look like for the user based on the recurring tasks they've specified.

The user should be able to go in any time and adjust allocated time for a task which would then recompute the future schedule.

The user should be able to put a pause on things if they want or need to focus on a single project with reminders from the app to return to their recurring tasks so as not to lose their compounding benefit. Highlight the importance of compounding in this scenario.

Tasks should have a specifiable level of urgency at different time scales. For example the user could specify a task as non-urgent after 1-3 days of doing it, then urgent after 3-6 days, then maybe dire emergency 7 days+. The app should intelligently prioritize the most urgent tasks based on this type of user specification. 

Basically the goal is to optimize spacing of various subjects so that the user can optimize their enjoyment, appreciation, and learning of them, while also respecting the beneficial compounding effect that is developed over a long period of time. My belief is that either you, in a sense, "become" something by doing it every single day (in which you become a master simply through mass repetition), or you master something by respecting your brains need for rest and recover, and complete tasks in that field once every 2-5 days, thereby optimizing your rest but also enabling the compounding effect

Some examples of tasks:

- Learning how to use relays in electronic circuits (1 hour every 2-5 days) until good understanding
- Learn Linear algebra for roughly 2 hours once every 2-4 days; higher priority (indefinitely until cleared)
- Study Chemistry for roughly 2 hours once every 2-4 days; lower priority
- Work on a web app 2-4 hours every day
- Do organization of my disorganized objects once every 7-14 days (indefinitely)
- Change oil in car once every 3-6 months (indefinitely)
- Car maintenance check once every 1-2 months
- Household / shop / garage organization audit once every 1-2 months
- File taxes
- Update credit card information across multiple sites


These are some example tasks that hopefully give you an idea of what I'm looking for. Tasks should have approximate completion times so that they can be added to the daily schedule allotting for appropriate times. There should be some sort of plus / minus when specifying both daily time allotment for all tasks and for the individual tasks themselves

Auto detect when too many projects are being taken on and warn the user they are pushing the limit. For example if all the tasks are being pushed to their last acceptable day (2-5 days would be the 5th day), then adding more tasks would further strain this and could even lead to tasks being pushed outside of the range.
Start and stop button for working on tasks. Guiding the user logically to the next task they should be working on that day.

Should be options for adding sections. For example you should be able to add a "Work" section and a "Play" section. You should be able to assign, or have the AI intelligently assign for you to which section. You should be able to specify how much time (roughly) each section should take each day. Another possible section could be "Relax". These are just examples, the user should name them how they want.
